name: Gbank
description_short: FDIC-insured bank offering business and personal checking/savings accounts, SBA loans, business financing, and a gaming-focused rewards credit card.
description_long:
GBank is a federally insured bank (Member FDIC) that operates as a full-service financial institution serving both business and personal customers. The bank positions itself as a relationship-focused lender with expertise in small business financing and growth-stage companies. Founded on principles of innovation and customer service, GBank emphasizes accessibility and digital-first banking alongside traditional in-person support.

GBank's service portfolio includes tiered personal and business checking/savings accounts (Presidential, Premier, Executive, Enterprise tiers), money market accounts, certificates of deposit, and debit cards. On the lending side, they offer SBA 7(a) and 504 loan programs, USDA B&I financing, commercial real estate loans, equipment financing, business lines of credit, and professional practice lending. They also issue the GBank Visa Signature credit card featuring 1% cashback on gaming/sports app loads and 2% on other purchases. Digital banking tools include mobile deposit, Zelle transfers, cash management, merchant services, and positive pay features.

GBank differentiates itself through specialized lending programs (SBA, USDA, professional practice, P&D/linehaul financing) and a gaming-industry-focused credit card product. The bank emphasizes flexibility in SBA lending and positions itself as a "bank for business at every stage." They provide educational resources through GBank University and offer accessibility features via AudioEye technology.

As a traditional bank, GBank's strength lies in relationship-based business lending and FDIC insurance protection. However, the website provides limited transparency on specific loan terms, APRs, minimum deposits, account fees, or interest rates. Geographic service area and branch locations are not clearly detailed. The gaming-focused credit card, while unique, has a narrow rewards niche outside that category. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Business checking accounts (Presidential, Prestige, Executive, Integrity NOW, IOLTA NOW tiers)
Personal checking accounts (Preferred, Premier, Enterprise, Liberty NOW tiers)
Business and personal savings accounts with tiered options
Money market accounts and certificates of deposit for both business and personal
SBA 7(a) and 504 loan programs for small business financing
USDA B&I loan program for agricultural/rural business financing
Commercial real estate loans and equipment financing
Business lines of credit and accounts receivable/inventory financing
Professional practice and medical lending specialization
GBank Visa Signature credit card with gaming/sports rewards
Mobile and digital banking with mobile deposit and remote capture
Cash management, merchant card services, and business courier services
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. government, providing strong account protection up to $250K per depositor
Comprehensive SBA lending programs (7(a) and 504) with stated flexible approval approach for small businesses
Specialized lending products including USDA B&I, professional practice loans, and P&D/linehaul financing rarely offered by smaller banks
Digital-first banking with mobile deposit, Zelle integration, and online account management across business and personal products
Tiered account structures (Presidential/Premier/Enterprise) allowing customers to match accounts to their activity level
Dual rewards credit card with targeted 1% cashback on gaming/sports merchants and 2% on general purchases
Accessibility-focused website design with AudioEye toolbar support for disabled users
cons: Website lacks transparency on specific loan terms, APRs, minimum loan amounts, or processing timelines for SBA/business products
No clear information on account fees, minimum balances, or interest rates for checking, savings, or money market products
Geographic service limitations not disclosed; unclear whether GBank operates nationally or regionally despite 'nationwide lender' claim
Gaming-focused credit card rewards structure has narrow appeal outside gaming/sports app loading merchant category
Limited customer review or rating information provided on the website itself

contact.address: 9115 W Russell Rd, Las Vegas, NV 89148

best_for: Small business owners seeking SBA or specialized commercial lending with relationship-based underwriting
Gaming industry professionals and sports fans wanting category-specific credit card rewards
Business owners at multiple growth stages (startups through established companies) wanting flexible lending options
Customers prioritizing FDIC insurance protection and federal regulatory oversight over fintech alternatives
diagnosis: GBank is best suited for small-to-mid-size business owners and entrepreneurs seeking relationship-based SBA lending, specialized commercial financing, and FDIC-protected deposit accounts. The primary caveat is lack of transparent pricing and terms on the website—customers must contact the bank directly to understand specific loan rates, fees, and qualification requirements before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 9115 W Russell Rd, Las Vegas, NV 89148
